title: SaplingIvkDecryptor::new validates Fr but stores bytes; subsequent Fr reconstruction not constant-time
description: scanner.rs SaplingIvkDecryptor::new calls jubjub::Fr::from_repr to validate ivk_bytes, discards the Fr, then stores only the raw bytes. On each try_decrypt_sapling call, Fr::from_repr is called again to reconstruct the Fr. The second call is done via .into_option().expect() which panics if the bytes are somehow no longer valid (impossible if the struct was constructed correctly, but note the bytes are in a Zeroizing wrapper — if zeroize partially runs on drop and the struct is used after that, this would panic). More practically, the Fr reconstruction on every output is O(notes_in_block) extra field operations. Already tracked as nie-br9a.6 (SaplingIvkDecryptor reconstructs Fr on every note) — filing as reminder that this is the right place to cache the PreparedIncomingViewingKey.
